title: The Influence Graph: Mapping the DNA of Code
overview: A directed acyclic graph (DAG) that maps how programming languages and concepts have inspired, derived from, or reacted to each other.
historical_context: The technical foundation of the Language Atlas, derived from decades of academic research and industry documentation.
mental_model: A family tree for languages, where multiple ancestors can contribute to a single descendant.
key_innovations: Weighted influence scores based on connection density.
tradeoffs: Influence is often subjective and can be debated by historians; the graph represents the Atlas’s synthesized perspective.
legacy: Powers the visualization and discovery engines across all Atlas interfaces.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Explain how the influence_score is calculated.
Trace the influence of ALGOL 60 on modern imperative languages.
